content.problemStatement:
CodeChef Inc. owns a rectangular piece of land sized w×h.  Its edges are parallel to the axes, with the bottom-left corner at (0,0) and top-right corner at (w,h).  They intend to build a new head office within this land.  The new office will be a square whose center is located at a point with integer coordinates, and whose diagonals are parallel to the axes and have length 2*d.

Unfortunately, there are n barricades on the land, some of which may need to be removed in order for the office to be built.  The (i)th barricade is located at (Xi, Yi) and will cost Ci to remove.  The office may only be built once all points it covers (including the boundaries) are free of barricades.


RequestHelp them find the minimum total cost of barricade removal.
content.inputFormat: First line contains four integers w, h, d, and n, representing the width and height of the land, the half-length of the diagonal of the square office, and the number of barricades, respectively. Each of the following n lines contains three integers Xi, Yi, and Ci, representing the x-coordinate, y-coordinate, and cost of the ith barricade, respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the minimum total cost of barricade removal on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The head office can be built at position (2, 2) with a cost of 2.
The head office can be built at position (3, 3) with a cost of 60.
There are no barricades, so the cost is 0.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by iterating over all possible positions of the square office and calculating the total cost of barricade removal for each position. The position with the minimum cost is the answer. The time complexity is O(n * w * h) because for each position, we need to check all barricades.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n * w * h) per test case
